JKH.chairman_outlook: The Group approaches the future with confidence and optimism as landmark investments begin to yield results. The nation is on the path towards a sustained recovery with prevailing socioeconomic stability providing a strong foundation to accelerate business growth and broader economic expansion. Following a prolonged period of uncertainty, these improvements will enable driving greater investor confidence, economic resilience, and a sustained long term recovery.

KPHL.chairman_outlook: The horizon is bright as we harness untapped opportunities in Sri Lanka's underpenetrated e-commerce market, where immense potential awaits. Domestically, we're set to capture greater market share through targeted expansions while internationally, Kapruka is flourishing as the premier e-distributor for iconic Sri Lankan brands on platforms like Amazon, eBay, and Walmart. This global momentum not only diversifies our revenue but also elevates Kapruka as a worldwide powerhouse, promising exciting growth trajectories for our shareholders.

COMB.chairman_outlook: While the outlook has improved with Sri Lanka's economic normalisation, sustained progress will require continued fiscal discipline, structural reforms and renewed private sector dynamism. Commercial Bank stands ready to play its part by preserving capital strength and liquidity leadership, continuing disciplined yet proactive private sector credit expansion aligned to productive sectors, and deepening digital transformation while strengthening cybersecurity. By combining prudence, innovation and leadership, Commercial Bank is well positioned not only to participate in Sri Lanka's post-crisis growth story, but to help shape it responsibly.

TJL.chairman_outlook: Teejay Lanka PLC is strategically positioned to achieve its long-term revenue target of USD 300 million through innovation, digitalisation, ESG excellence, and market expansion. Despite challenges from macroeconomic volatility and geopolitical risks, the company's diversified footprint, innovation-led mindset, and stakeholder-centric approach will enable it to overcome obstacles and capitalise on emerging opportunities including the China Plus One strategy. The company remains focused on building an agile, purpose-driven organisation ready to lead in a dynamic global landscape.

ACME.chairman_outlook: The Company remains committed to expanding its export footprint through resilient supply chain strategies, investment in quality assurance systems, and customer-focused innovation. As a responsible company, we will continue to explore and invest in environmentally sustainable packaging technologies, with the aim of minimizing environmental impact while maintaining product integrity and performance. These efforts are aimed at positioning the organisation as a preferred partner in international markets, while contributing to the broader national objective of strengthening Sri Lanka's export economy.

HNB.chairman_outlook: Continuing the momentum of the past two years, the Sri Lankan economy is expected to expand by around 4-5% in 2026, yet the experience of the 2022 crisis underscores the importance of sustained resilience requiring stronger economic buffers, reinforced policy credibility, and enhanced capacity to absorb future shocks. HNB is well positioned to energise Sri Lanka's future through the Strategic Plan for 2026-2030, leveraging strong fundamentals and disciplined governance to continue strengthening core business verticals while reinforcing the Bank's position as a fully fledged financial services conglomerate.

We anticipate the positive growth observed in 2024 to continue, and we aim to increase business volumes and achieve sustainable profitability while maintaining our status as the nation's most esteemed brand.

DIAL.chairman_outlook: Dialog enters 2025 with renewed strength and optimism as a more resilient, innovative, and future-ready enterprise after successfully weathering unprecedented challenges. Our priorities remain clear: expanding digital services, deepening sustainability practices, and delivering value to all stakeholders through continued investment in next-generation technologies and a forward-looking approach. With the Airtel Lanka integration complete and strong financial positioning, Dialog is poised to shape the next chapter of Sri Lanka's digital evolution and lead the country's transformation into a regional digital hub.

The Company remains committed to strengthening bonds with all stakeholders while maintaining the highest standards of transparency, risk management, and long-term value creation.

HVA.chairman_outlook: The company's focus for 2025/26 will be continued execution of diversification and customer consolidation strategy, with tea flavouring and herbal segments scaled up to capture more value in traditional and new markets. Full-year benefits of restructured finance function and cost optimization programmes are eagerly awaited, while focus on domestic market through targeted retail, modern trade and tourism-led initiatives will be accelerated. Unless significant and unforeseen global disruptions arise, this strategy remains HVA Foods' path to regain profitability and deliver sustained value to shareholders.

JINS.chairman_outlook: The Board is confident in the Company's optimistic five-year growth plan. We remain firmly focused on strengthening our position as a leading force in the life insurance industry, guided by uncompromising standards of financial integrity and sustainable value creation.

ATLL.chairman_outlook: As the nation enters a new phase of economic growth, Amana Takaful Life is committed to supporting individuals and businesses with innovative, future-ready insurance solutions. We will focus on digital transformation, invest in advanced IT infrastructure, and expand market penetration across Sri Lanka while maintaining our role as a trusted partner in protection and financial security. By continuously evolving and adapting, we aim to strengthen our position in the recovering economic landscape.

DOCK.chairman_outlook: We remain cautiously optimistic and are guided by a forward-looking strategy aimed at strengthening our presence in high-growth sectors while deepening regional integration and expanding infrastructure to support long-term sustainable growth. Despite facing one of the most challenging years in company history, we successfully navigated global uncertainties by focusing on innovation, specialization, and operational agility, positioning ourselves to transform challenges into opportunities as we continue shaping the maritime industry in Sri Lanka and beyond.

SCAP.chairman_outlook: We remain cautiously optimistic about the year ahead with emerging stability in inflation, interest rates, and exchange rate providing a more predictable foundation for the financial services industry. Our strategic focus will centre on deepening digital transformation, enhancing capital efficiency, strengthening risk governance, and expanding product and distribution capabilities across businesses. We will continue to invest in technology, data analytics, and human capital to ensure our companies remain agile, innovative, and future-ready while maintaining our commitment to operational excellence and customer trust.

SIRA.chairman_outlook: Building on strong performance this year, we are confident in our ability to sustain and accelerate growth in the near future. With improvements in the macroeconomic environment, we are strategically positioned to seize emerging opportunities across both domestic and international markets, with enhanced focus on export portfolio expansion anticipated to create an avenue for entry into untapped global markets. We will continue to leverage deep industry expertise and advanced technologies, positioning ourselves as a trusted and quality-driven cable and wire manufacturer.

NDB.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economy has transitioned to a new era of growth after prolonged stress, with NDB positioned to be a key catalyst in the country's journey by prioritising capital flows towards high-impact sectors. ATL.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economic recovery gained traction in 2024 with three consecutive quarters of positive GDP growth and declining inflation. The newly elected leadership provided policy stability, reinforcing investor and consumer confidence. Amana Takaful stands ready to support individuals and businesses with innovative, future-ready insurance solutions as Sri Lanka embarks on its next phase of economic growth.

BERU.chairman_outlook: The outlook for Sri Lanka's tourism industry is optimistic, with 3 million arrivals targeted for 2025 surpassing pre-pandemic benchmarks. As we approach the winter season traditionally our strongest period we do so with optimism and confidence.

BFN.chairman_outlook: Janashakthi Finance steps into the new financial year with a bold and ambitious strategic plan, anchored in resilience, innovation and strategic foresight. We remain open, agile, and resolutely focused on our growth mandate while evaluating competitive landscape for strategic opportunities.

BUKI.chairman_outlook: The global economic outlook remains uncertain, shaped by shifting trade dynamics and broader geopolitical and financial volatility. Within this context, the Group remains committed to driving value creation through disciplined execution of strategic priorities, focusing on enhancing operational performance, fostering innovation, and strengthening engagement across all business areas. Backed by a strong foundation and talented team, the Group is well positioned to deliver sustainable growth and long-term value for all stakeholders.

CALH.chairman_outlook: From stabilization to sustainable growth: A renewed commitment to Sri Lanka's future. The crisis that defined the years prior has not been forgotten, yet it is increasingly clear that Sri Lanka is beginning to emerge from its most difficult period with resilience, reform, and responsibility at the heart of its approach. CAL has remained a steadfast institution, fully aligned with the national goal of building a more resilient and forward-looking economy.

CCS.chairman_outlook: The emergence of strong macroeconomic fundamentals alongside a credible platform for sustained, broad-based growth marks a notable shift in Sri Lanka's economic landscape. Low and stable inflation, favourable interest rate environment, improved market sentiment, and sustained socio-economic stability are expected to improve household income and boost discretionary spending. Although downside risks remain from global geopolitical tensions, the business is well positioned to capitalise on this growth momentum with improving consumer confidence supporting sustained volume growth.

FCT.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka enters a long-needed phase of consolidation with strong political capital, CBSL independence fortified and new Public Debt Management Office in place. We anticipate continued yield curve flattening with rates consolidating in 5-10% corridor over next five years, requiring strategic pivot from opportunistic trading to sustainable yield-oriented positioning centered on market making.

GEST.chairman_outlook: The year under review was another remarkable year thanks to efforts of all staff and stakeholders, with Group turnover reaching Rs. 1,753.4 million and profit of Rs. 103.4 million representing 14.89% growth. GRAN.chairman_outlook: The Chairman remains optimistic that Sri Lanka's economy will strengthen its macroeconomic stability as the government continues implementing necessary fiscal reforms and managing inflationary pressures under the IMF programme. HSIG.chairman_outlook: The global tourism industry is poised for sustained growth in 2025/26, with AI-driven personalisation, increased investment in sustainable tourism infrastructure, and growing middle class in emerging markets as key growth drivers. However, the sector will need to navigate challenges such as environmental impact, workforce shortages, and geopolitical volatility. Hotels that focus on innovation, customer-centric experiences, and ESG practices are likely to lead the way in shaping a more resilient tourism landscape.

LLUB.chairman_outlook: As the nation progresses towards economic stability in 2025, following the crisis experienced in 2022/2023 and bolstered by a recovery in key sectors, the Company remains confident about the potential for further growth and stability in the local market. On the export front considering the recent political and economic turmoil in Bangladesh and Maldives, we remain cautiously optimistic of revenue growth in both export markets.

LOLC.chairman_outlook: LOLC's vision is underpinned by our commitment to inclusive growth, global relevance, and sustainability. LVEF.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economy has made a remarkable recovery in 2024, surpassing growth expectations by recording a 5.0% growth, compared to the projected 4.4%. In 2025, growth is expected to moderate to 3.5%, reflecting scarring effects of the crisis and structural impediments to growth, amid global headwinds and unprecedented trade policy uncertainty. The government is focusing on fiscal sustainability, aiming to improve the Revenue-to-GDP ratio, reduce the budget deficit, and maintain a primary surplus.

LWL.chairman_outlook: Lanka Walltiles is navigating one of the most challenging periods in its operational history, marked by internal restructuring, shifting market dynamics, and global economic uncertainty. The strategy prioritises restoring profitability through operational efficiency, product realignment, and disciplined financial management while leveraging emerging opportunities in export markets, particularly the USA and Canada. Guided by commitment to innovation, sustainability, and talent development, this plan seeks to transition Lanka Walltiles from recovery to sustainable growth, setting a clear course for long-term competitiveness and value creation.

NAMU.chairman_outlook: The Company remains committed to advancing with clarity, discipline, and a long-term vision that delivers enduring value to all stakeholders. Guided by the Triple Bottom Line - People, Planet, and Profit - we strive to integrate sustainability into every aspect of our business while ensuring long-term profitability. Despite challenges, shareholder returns have strengthened, reflecting our commitment to delivering value.

REEF.chairman_outlook: The outlook for FY 2025/26 is one of cautious optimism with potential resurgence of Chinese market, stabilisation in India post-election and improvements in global air connectivity pointing towards more favourable operating environment. However, geopolitical uncertainties and potential resurgence of global health threats could pose downside risks. Focus will remain on completing refurbishment of key assets, expanding experiential offerings, improving brand positioning and achieving operational profitability.

TAFL.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economic outlook for 2025 is projected to strengthen further, supported by ongoing fiscal reforms, increased tourist arrivals, and growing foreign worker remittances, which will help drive demand for poultry products. The potential to explore export opportunities, particularly in value-added and sustainable poultry, remains promising, offering new avenues for growth and diversification. TAF expects to significantly increase investment towards renewable energy and sustainable farming practices to mitigate climate-related risks and enhance long-term resilience.

VFIN.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economy demonstrating resilience in 2024 with 5% real GDP growth provides a stronger platform for growth. Vallibel Finance remains focused on continued growth and impact, committed to creating a lasting legacy that goes beyond financial milestones and fosters long-term trust within the industry. We are incredibly optimistic about the future as sustained efforts align with contributing meaningfully to Sri Lanka's socio-economic development.

VLL.chairman_outlook: The company is well positioned to support much-needed change in the renewable energy industry by leveraging years of expertise and growing portfolio with network of partners. Sri Lanka's economy has performed notably better than most debt-distressed countries with 5% growth expansion, and cheaper debt finance with recent downward trend of interest rates supports developers in the energy industry.

VONE.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka marked a decisive shift toward macroeconomic stabilisation with broad-based sectoral growth, declining inflation, and improving investor confidence. The Group remained agile and strategically focused, leveraging its diversified portfolio to navigate complexities while maintaining steady consolidated revenue of LKR 122.8 billion. Moving forward, we are well-positioned to strengthen our business model through strategic investments, deeper ESG integration, and digital transformation while being guided by a timeless vision and driven by boundless potential.

VPEL.chairman_outlook: The renewable energy sector is rapidly emerging as a cornerstone of the global shift toward sustainable innovation. With Sri Lanka's economic recovery and renewed investor confidence generating momentum, clean energy is positioned at the forefront of this progress. We will continue to pursue growth while remaining agile in a rapidly evolving market, backed by strong balance sheet and clear sense of purpose.

WIND.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's renewable energy sector holds immense potential but faces challenges from macroeconomic volatility and regulatory execution delays. WindForce remains proactive in exploring innovative financing solutions while expanding internationally, particularly in Africa and Pakistan, leveraging technological focus on solar power. The national commitment to 70% renewables by 2030 provides crucial growth drivers, positioning WindForce for sustained expansion and enhanced shareholder value.

ASHO.chairman_outlook: Looking ahead, the outlook for the commercial transport sector remains positive. The recovery in economic activity, rising infrastructure investments, and the easing of interest rates are expected to sustain demand for commercial vehicles across both goods and passenger mobility segments. With a dominant position in the bus segment and expanding presence across light and heavy trucks, Lanka Ashok Leyland is strategically positioned to capitalise on these trends.

COCR.chairman_outlook: Sri Lanka's economic recovery has been supported by government measures and Central Bank interventions with significant progress made in macroeconomic stability. HNB.X.chairman_outlook: Continuing the momentum of the past two years, the Sri Lankan economy is expected to expand by around 4-5% in 2026. The period ahead calls for stronger economic buffers, reinforced policy credibility, and enhanced capacity to absorb future shocks. Anchored by strong fundamentals, disciplined governance, and a 137-year legacy of trust, HNB is well positioned to energise Sri Lanka's future.

HNBF.X.chairman_outlook: We remain cautiously optimistic as Sri Lanka's macroeconomic fundamentals begin to improve with signs of recovery becoming evident including easing inflation, declining interest rates, and emerging growth opportunities. With a fortified capital base, rebalanced secure lending portfolio, and ongoing focus on strong governance and inclusive growth, we find ourselves well-positioned to act as both catalyst and beneficiary of the country's next phase of economic revival while maintaining unwavering commitment to responsible innovation, stakeholder value, and national development.

LGL.X.chairman_outlook: As Sri Lanka continues its journey of economic recovery, the LAUGFS Gas Group is committed to being a reliable partner in the Country's progress through transparent governance practices, adherence to evolving regulatory standards, and consistent delivery of reliable, safe, and customer-centric energy solutions. While uniquely positioned to support national energy security through import terminal operations and LPG vessel fleet, the Group plans to optimize terminal infrastructure and accelerate regional operations.

Electricity Act No. 36 of 2024 enacted in June 2024 with several amendments still undergoing legislative process
Recognition needed by CEB and Ministry of Power and Energy of private developers' track record for enhanced project execution flexibility
Compliance with Central Environmental Authority, Mahaweli Authority and regional authorities in Sri Lanka
Adherence to National Environment Management Authority and Ministry of Energy requirements in Uganda
segment_info: Overseas hydro segment recorded Rs. 2.2 billion revenue and Rs. 904 million profit with Muvumbe and Bukinda projects in Uganda
Local hydro segment profit of Rs. 759 million vs Rs. 420 million prior year including CEB compensation claims settlement
EPC segment delivered Rs. 2.1 billion revenue and Rs. 312 million profit from successful Guyana contract completion
Dendro & Plantation segment contributed Rs. 104 million profit with Rs. 440 million revenue representing 22% increase
Solar segment recorded Rs. 91 million profit improvement from Rs. 59 million previous year reflecting operational efficiencies
Engineering, Consultancy, Project Management segment recorded Rs. 100 million profit from Rs. 184 million revenue
all_risks: Counterparty credit risk with utilities CEB, LECO and UETCL for electricity payments under Power Purchase Agreements
Financial risk from liquidity challenges and debt servicing obligations affecting shareholder wealth
Economic risk from macroeconomic conditions, interest rates, exchange rates and political stability affecting investments
Interest rate risk from fluctuations impacting company profitability
Liquidity risk from difficulties meeting short-term financial obligations
Exchange rate risk from currency fluctuations affecting foreign operations and equipment imports
Health and safety risk to employees during construction and operational phases at power plants
Environmental and disaster risk from potential adverse events disrupting operations
Epidemic risk of operational and economic impact from pandemic outbreaks
Weather risk of disruptions and financial losses due to changing weather conditions affecting generation
Public relations risk of losing reputation leading to stakeholder conflicts
Stakeholder risk from failure to adequately manage stakeholder expectations
Operational risk of losses from internal activities, processes and procedures
Fraud risk from suboptimal use of company assets and corruption
Information technology risk from unauthorized access and data security breaches
Regulatory and legal risk from changes in laws and regulations materially impacting business
Competition risk from failure to adapt to changing business environment amid increased renewable energy sector competition
Country and political risk collection from investing in foreign countries including Uganda and Guyana
Geographical and diversification risk from exposure to specific locations and insufficient market spread
